title: scabiosa triandra subsp. agrestis
intro: Scabiosa triandra subsp. agrestis, also known as Wild Scabious, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the honeysuckle family, native to Europe and Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and heal wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant is rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which contribute to its health benefits, including its antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, as well as its ability to aid in digestion and relieve menstrual cramps. Wild Scabious is typically prepared as a tea or infusion by steeping the flowers and leaves in hot water, or as a tincture by extracting the plant's medicinal compounds with a solvent.
